title: Ctenium cirrosum
intro: Ctenium cirrosum, also known as Bird's Tongue Grass, is a perennial grass native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its distinctive, tongue-shaped leaves and feathery inflorescences. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ctenium cirrosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is also used to make a decoction, which is used to treat coughs and colds.
taxonomy: Ctenium cirrosum is a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Ctenium, which is a group of grasses commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Ctenium cirrosum is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Tracheophyta, and class Liliopsida. The order of classification for this species is Poales, and its family is Poaceae. Ctenium cirrosum is a member of the subfamily Chloridoideae.
uses: Ctenium cirrosum is a plant species known for its potential medicinal properties.
